definition: Consciousness arising dependent on the mental faculty and dhammas.
notes: The project prefers mind-consciousness because it preserves the analytical relation between mano and viññāṇa without collapsing the term into thought. Mental consciousness is acceptable in explanatory prose when the compound needs a slightly smoother English shape.

context_rules.context: In most analytical and source-facing contexts
In explanatory prose where the compound reads more naturally with a fuller adjective

example_phrases.pali: mano ca paṭicca dhamme ca uppajjati manoviññāṇaṃ
example_phrases.translation: dependent on the mind and dhammas, mind-consciousness arises
